description: Figure 3 presents a grouped bar chart comparing the specific stiffness of four materials—Aluminium (6xxx series), HSLA steel, Fe-8Al ferritic steel, and Fe-22Mn-8Al-0.8C austenitic steel—normalized against that of aluminium across three part-dependent indices: E/ρ for a tie (n = 1), √E/ρ for a beam (n = 1/2), and ∛E/ρ for a panel (n = 1/3). The y-axis denotes specific stiffness relative to aluminium. For the tie index, HSLA and aluminium exhibit the highest values (~1.0–1.03), followed by Fe-8Al (~0.93) and Fe-22Mn-8Al-0.8C (~0.87). Under the beam index, aluminium remains at 1.0, while HSLA and Fe-8Al drop to ~0.61, and Fe-22Mn-8Al-0.8C falls to ~0.58. For the panel index, aluminium again leads at 1.0, with Fe-8Al (~0.53), HSLA (~0.51), and Fe-22Mn-8Al-0.8C (~0.51) clustering at lower values. The figure demonstrates that the low-density steels do not surpass HSLA or aluminium in specific stiffness for any structural configuration. This supports the conclusion that weight-saving applications of Fe-Mn-Al-C steels must target components where stiffness is not the critical design constraint.
